title_cn: 美国、伊朗对霍尔木兹是否开放存在争议，油价上涨；市场重要一周|彭博简报 07/13/26

Investors prepare for Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh's testimony on Capitol Hill this week along with CPI and Wall Street earnings. Thierry Wizman of  Macquarie looks ahead to the busy week. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Wall Street Mounts Pushback on Trillion-Dollar Stablecoin Boom
title_cn: 华尔街对万亿美元稳定币热潮发起阻力
summary: For years, banks largely watched stablecoins evolve from a niche cryptocurrency product into a payments network moving tens of trillions of dollars annually. Now they’re dusting off the same collaborative playbook that produced Zelle, betting shared infrastructure is the best way to stop digital dollars from encroaching further on their business.

title: Russian Refinery Runs Plunge to Lowest in More Than Two Decades
title_cn: 俄罗斯炼油厂运行跌至二十多年来的最低水平
summary: A wave of Ukrainian attacks has pushed Russian refining runs to the lowest in more than 21 years, deepening a domestic fuel crunch and further squeezing the global market.

summary: A day after making an investment call tied to subsiding Middle East tensions, BCA Research’s Marko Papic had to scrap his recommendation at a loss. Donald Trump had said the war in Iran was back on.

title: US & Iran Trade Fresh Strikes, SK Hynix Shares Fall Most on Record | The Opening Trade 7/13/2026
title_cn: 美国和伊朗贸易新一轮打击，SK海力士股价创历史新高开盘交易 7/13/2026
summary:
Oil gained after the US carried out another wave of strikes against Iran, with the two sides disputing whether the Strait of Hormuz was open. Global benchmark Brent earlier approached $80, before paring some of the increase. The US on Sunday struck dozens of targets to degrade Iran’s ability to attack international shipping through the waterway. Iran said the strait would be closed “until further notice,” though Western navies insisted it remained open.

South Korea’s AI-fueled stock rally came under renewed pressure Monday as SK Hynix  tumbled by a record 15%, underscoring growing investor concerns that the boom had become overstretched. 

The Opening Trade has everything you need to know as markets open across Europe. With analysis you won't find anywhere else, we break down the biggest stories of the day and speak to top guests who have skin in the game. Hosted by Anna Edwards and Tom Mackenzie. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: India Trade Deficit Widens as Hormuz Risks Cloud Outlook
title_cn: 霍尔木兹海峡风险使印度贸易逆差扩大
summary: India’s trade deficit widened in June, with uncertainty over the US-Iran conflict and shipping through the Strait of Hormuz continuing to cloud the outlook for global supply chains.

summary: Chile’s government has a window of opportunity to sell debt abroad as sovereign spreads hover near two-decade lows, with investors shrugging off an escalation in the Middle East conflict, domestic debt woes and a stagnating economy.

title: Indonesia Avoids S&P Rating Outlook Cut in Win for Prabowo
title_cn: 印尼避免标普评级展望下调，普拉博沃胜出
summary: Indonesia retained its investment-grade rating and stable outlook at S&P Global Ratings, a reprieve for President Prabowo Subianto and his fiscal policy credibility after a bond market selloff in June.

title: Investors Expect ‘Sunshine and Rainbows’ This Earnings Season
title_cn: 投资者期待本财报季“阳光和彩虹”

summary: The Federal Reserve’s top bank cop warned the Financial Stability Board against enforcing “strict rules” that are not suitable for a diverse group of countries, saying those efforts can erode the international watchdog’s effectiveness.

summary:
"The Pulse With Francine Lacqua" is all about conversations with high profile guests in the beating heart of global business, economics, finance and politics. Based in London, we go wherever the story is, bringing you exclusive interviews and market-moving scoops.

Today's guests: Seema Shah, Principal Asset Management Chief Global Strategist; Cassander Verwey, JPMorgan EMEA Head of M&A  (Source: Bloomberg)

summary: Africa-focused data-center firm Raxio Group plans to enter Tanzania as it increases its funding pool to $380 million, Chief Executive Officer Robert Skjødt said.

title: TSMC’s Sales Soar 36% in Latest Sign of AI Spending Momentum

summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. reported a 36% jump in quarterly sales, meeting elevated expectations while signaling global demand for AI computing remains intact.

title: Xi to Debut at China’s Flagship AI Summit as US Rivalry Heats Up

title: Saudi Arabia Becomes Top Buyer in Taiwan’s Young Drone Industry
title_cn: 沙特阿拉伯成为台湾年轻无人机产业的最大买家
summary: Saudi Arabia bought a record $47.2 million of drones from Taiwan last month — a sign the island’s nascent UAV industry is becoming more attractive to buyers.

title: Why AI Might Actually Create More Work for Lawyers
title_cn: 为什么人工智能实际上可能为律师创造更多工作
summary: It seems obvious that among the many industries that AI might disrupt, the legal profession might face some of the most adverse outcomes. When clerical, research-based tasks like searching through databases and reading contracts are automated, what is left for lawyers to do and how might they justify all those billable hours? In this episode we speak with Gary Wingens, chair and partner at the law firm Lowenstein Sandler. He talks about how his firm is using AI and why he thinks the technology could end up increasing legal work for lawyers as costs come down, creating a sort of “Jevon's paradox” for lawsuits, deals and litigation. We also talk about the billable hours model and training junior talent. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Morgan Stanley’s Wilson Sees Profit Boost for Stocks Beyond Tech
title_cn: 摩根士丹利的威尔逊认为科技股以外的股票利润将得到提振
summary: US stocks beyond the technology giants are poised to report strong earnings this season, broadening the equity rally, according to Morgan Stanley strategists.

title: SK Hynix Shares Slump in South Korea After US Listing

summary: SK Hynix shares declined in Seoul on Monday after the memory chipmaker’s highly-anticipated US trading debut. The company’s $26.5 billion US offering was closely watched as a test of demand for overseas offerings as well as for the longevity of the artificial intelligence rally. Bloomberg's Neil Campling reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Hyundai Motor Workers Strike Over Bonuses, Robot Job Security
title_cn: 现代汽车工人因奖金和机器人工作保障而罢工
summary: Hyundai Motor Co. workers began a three-day partial strike on Monday as their union demands bigger bonuses following landmark deals at semiconductor companies, and seek guarantees jobs won’t be lost to AI and robots.

title: Netflix Combats Questions About Slowing Growth
title_cn: Netflix 回应有关增长放缓的问题
summary: Netflix’s growth is slowing. What comes next?

title: Apple’s M6, M7 and M8 Chips Show How AI Is Reshaping the Company
title_cn: 苹果的 M6、M7 和 M8 芯片展示了人工智能如何重塑公司
summary: Also: New Apple Pencils are coming.

title: OpenAI, Meta, SpaceXAI Compete for More Cost-Efficient AI Models
title_cn: OpenAI、Meta、SpaceXAI 争夺更具成本效益的人工智能模型
summary: Three prominent artificial intelligence developers released new models over the past week. They all promise to be more advanced, but their biggest immediate selling point may not be what they can do but how little they charge to do it.

title: Zhipu’s Chinese Founder Says Frontier AI Should Stay Open to All
title_cn: 智普创始人表示前沿人工智能应该向所有人开放
summary: The founder of Chinese AI lab Zhipu argued that frontier artificial intelligence should remain broadly accessible rather than controlled by select individuals, weighing in on a growing debate about the risks posed by ever more powerful models.

title: China EVs on Road Have Average Age of 1.8 Years, Newspaper Says
title_cn: 报纸称中国上路电动汽车平均车龄为 1.8 年
summary: Chinese electric vehicles on the road are on average just 1.8 years old, compared with 8.2 years for gasoline-powered cars, 21st Century Business Herald reported, citing a report by the China Association of Automobile Manufacturers and Hejun Consulting.

title: UK Counter-Terror Police Lead Probe Into Murder of Ann Widdecombe
title_cn: 英国反恐警察牵头调查安·维德科姆谋杀案
summary: UK Counter-terrorism police said they have taken over the investigation into the murder of Reform politician Ann Widdecombe after new evidence emerged about her death last week.

title: EU to Sanction Sudan’s Gold Trade as Civil War Rages
title_cn: 内战愈演愈烈，欧盟将制裁苏丹黄金贸易
summary: The European Union approved new trade prohibitions on Sudan’s gold sector as the country’s civil war enters its fourth year.

title: Putin Puts Akzo Nobel’s Russian Assets Under Temporary Control
title_cn: 普京将阿克苏诺贝尔的俄罗斯资产置于临时控制之下
summary: Russian President Vladimir Putin ordered stakes in three Russian companies owned by Dutch paints and coatings maker Akzo Nobel NV transferred to temporary administration.

title: DOJ Is Probing Claims UAW’s President Abused Union Position
title_cn: 司法部正在调查 UAW 主席滥用工会职位的指控
summary: The US Justice Department launched a grand jury investigation into allegations that the head of the United Auto Workers pressured another high-ranking union official to secure benefits for his fiancée and her sister, according to internal communications within the labor organization.

title: How the Sudden Death of Sen. Graham Will Shift the GOP
title_cn: 参议员格雷厄姆的突然去世将如何改变共和党
summary: Jon Lieber of the Eurasia Group reflects on how the sudden death of Senator Lindsey Graham of South Carolina will impact President Donald Trump and foreign policy in the Republican party. He speaks on "Bloomberg Surveillance." Graham died on Saturday at age 71. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: California Lawmakers Push Fix for Hollywood Tax Credits
title_cn: 加州立法者推动修复好莱坞税收抵免
summary: A California business tax credit cap aimed at saving $4 billion threatens to drive away film and television production, according to a letter signed by a bipartisan group of lawmakers who demanded a quick fix to the budget signed by Governor Gavin Newsom.

title: Hungary Is Poised to Oust President in Rollback of Orban Era
title_cn: 匈牙利准备罢黜总统，重返欧尔班时代
summary: Hungarian lawmakers are set to oust the Viktor Orban-allied president as Prime Minister Peter Magyar seeks a clean break from his nationalist predecessor.

title: Macron Calls on EU to Deliver More Joint Defense Projects
title_cn: 马克龙呼吁欧盟实施更多联合防御项目
summary: French President Emmanuel Macron urged European Union allies to pursue more joint defense projects as he sought to turn the page on a failed partnership with Germany to build a new fighter jet.

title: Morgan Stanley’s $2.5 Billion Parking-Meter Sale Roils Chicago Council
title_cn: 摩根士丹利 25 亿美元出售停车计时器引发芝加哥议会骚乱
summary: For almost two decades, Chicago has been stewing over a $1.15 billion deal to lease 36,000 parking meters to an investor group led by Morgan Stanley. Now, with the meters set to change hands, the city council is seizing on an opening to push back.

title: Graham’s Death Leaves Void for Trump, Global Allies
title_cn: 格雷厄姆的去世给特朗普和全球盟友留下了空白
summary: On Ukraine, Iran and more, Graham held Trump’s ear like few others.

title: UK Civil Servant Challenges Firing Over Mandelson Appointment
title_cn: 英国公务员挑战解雇曼德尔森任命
summary: The former head of the UK Foreign Office, who was fired by Keir Starmer in the row over Peter Mandelson’s links to Jeffrey Epstein, has submitted a legal challenge against the government.

title: Uproar Over High Doctors Wages Hits Polish Ruling Party Support
title_cn: 医生高薪引起的轩然大波打击了波兰执政党的支持
summary: Prime Minister Donald Tusk’s ruling party is losing ground in polls a year before elections, following uproar over how much doctors’ salaries are eating up from the strained healthcare budget.

title: Ukraine Says It Hit More Oil and Dry-Cargo Ships in Sea of Azov
title_cn: 乌克兰称在亚速海击中更多油船和干货船
summary: Ukrainian forces hit seven Russian fuel tankers, five dry-cargo vessels and a batch of other ships in the Sea of Azov as Kyiv expands the scope of its maritime attacks in the area.

title: Europe Looks for a Musk-Free Path to Space
title_cn: 欧洲寻找一条无麝香的太空之路
summary: Europe is competing with US, China and Russia to establish a sovereign space industry.

title: Long Night of Jakarta Raids Found Gold Bars, Wads of Cash
title_cn: 雅加达长夜突袭发现金条和大量现金
summary: Diners at de’Clan Signature, an eatery in the low-rise South Jakarta neighborhood of Cipete, knew something was up when heavily armed police converged upon the restaurant last Wednesday afternoon.

title: As Milei’s Aura Fades, Argentina Starts to Look for a Third Way
title_cn: 米莱光环褪去，阿根廷开始寻找第三条路
summary: Corruption was once Javier Milei’s strongest card against the Peronist movement that dominated Argentine politics for decades. Now it’s just one of several vulnerabilities that are damaging the libertarian president’s brand and giving wings to the opposition’s comeback plans.

title: Zelenskyy Removes Prime Minister as Allies Meet in Paris
title_cn: 盟友在巴黎举行会议时，泽伦斯基罢免了总理
summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said he planned to replace Prime Minister Yuliia Svyrydenko as part of a broader change in “political strategy.” Zelenskyy is expected in Paris on Monday for a meeting of the so-called coalition of the willing, where Ukraine's allies are likely to discuss more aid for Kyiv including for air defense. Bloomberg's Oliver Crook reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Burnham Weighs Bigger Budget as Experts Lobby on Economy
title_cn: 专家游说经济，伯纳姆考虑增加预算
summary: Andy Burnham, the UK’s presumptive next prime minister, is considering unveiling a bolstered budget later this year as allies and experts seek to persuade him to pursue a land tax, public control of utilities and a more ambitious devolution strategy.

title: South Africa Says About 53,500 Migrants Returned or Deported in Crackdown
title_cn: 南非称大约 53,500 名移民在镇压中返回或被驱逐出境
summary: South Africa processed almost 53,500 foreign nationals for deportation or voluntary repatriation since last month, as the authorities act on pledges to speed up removals while warning vigilante groups to stop conducting illegal searches for undocumented foreigners.

title: Singapore Court Postpones French Teen’s Straw-Licking Case to July 30
title_cn: 新加坡法院将法国青少年舔吸管案推迟至 7 月 30 日审理
summary: SINGAPORE (AP) — A court case involving a French teen charged for filming himself licking a straw from a vending machine and then putting it back was postponed Monday as prosecutors sought to clarify if he could continue his studies if convicted.

title: Yen Falls on Report Japan Has No Plans to Change GPIF Allocation
title_cn: 日元下跌，因有报道称日本没有计划改变 GPIF 分配
summary: The yen weakened and Japanese government bonds extended losses after Reuters reported that Japan has no plans to overhaul the Government Pension Investment Fund’s asset allocation.

title: What a Europe-Led NATO Would Look Like
title_cn: 欧洲领导的北约会是什么样子
summary: US allies no longer believe they can count on Washington to stand with them against Russia. They’re now urgently debating what it would take to go it alone.

title: France to Summon Russia Ambassador Over Cyber Attacks
title_cn: 法国因网络攻击传唤俄罗斯大使
summary: France will summon Russia’s ambassador in the coming days regarding a vast cyberattack and spying campaign in several European countries, Foreign Affairs Minister Jean-Noel Barrot said.

title: Fresh US-Iran Strikes Threaten Ceasefire; TMSC Sales Surge | Daybreak Europe 7/13/2026
title_cn: 美伊新一轮袭击威胁停火； TMSC 销售激增 |黎明欧洲 7/13/2026
summary:
Bloomberg Daybreak Europe is your essential morning viewing to stay ahead. Live from London, we set the agenda for your day, catching you up with overnight markets news from the US and Asia. And we'll tell you what matters for investors in Europe, giving you insight before trading begins.

On today's show, oil jumped after the US and Iran traded fresh strikes. US Central Command says dozens of targets were hit in one of the heaviest bombardments since an interim deal to end the war was signed in June. Tehran retaliated with attacks on US allies in the Persian Gulf and beyond.

SK Hynix shares fell more than 13% in Seoul, after its highly-anticipated US trading debut. The slump drove the benchmark Kospi 8% lower, triggering a brief circuit-breaker suspension. In Taiwan, TSMC reported a 36% jump in quarterly sales, in a sign that global demand for AI hardware remains intact.

Today's guest: Lale Akoner, eToro, Global Market Analyst. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Moscow Faces Second Day of Mass Ukrainian Drone Attacks
title_cn: 莫斯科面临乌克兰无人机大规模袭击的第二天
summary: Moscow faced a second day of large-scale Ukrainian drone attacks, with officials reporting hundreds of the unmanned craft flying toward the Russian capital and three fatalities in the surrounding region.

title: US and Iran Trade Fresh Strikes
title_cn: 美国和伊朗进行新的打击
summary: The US and Iran exchanged fresh strikes overnight into Monday as they continued their tit-for-tat attacks while issuing conflicting declarations over whether the Strait of Hormuz was open to shipping. Bloomberg's Abeer Abu Omar reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Malaysia’s Anwar Weakened by Ally’s Landslide in Johor Elections
title_cn: 马来西亚安华因盟友在柔佛选举中获得压倒性胜利而削弱
summary: Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim’s grip over Malaysia‘s ruling alliance took a hit over the weekend after coalition partner Barisan Nasional swept elections in Johor, strengthening the former ruling bloc’s hand as the government weighs whether to call an early general election.
